anthropic.note: Enforced via daily lockfile /tmp/audit_check_ai_last_run.txt — at most one audit fire per UTC day. Each fire = 1 Opus extended-thinking call (~$2). No Haiku callers in active code; if reintroduced, pair with explicit enforcement and update this cap.

gemini.note: Hard-coded in gemma4_helper.py _GEMINI_DAILY_LIMIT (added 2026-04-25). Currently only audit_check + audit_fire_debate use it; cap has plenty of headroom.

ai_consensus.gemini:
**(a) Runaway Loops**: Anthropic’s cap relies on `/tmp/`. If a container restarts or the OS clears `/tmp`, the lockfile vanishes. A retry-storm on any of the 24 unmapped timers will trigger infinite $2 Opus calls.

**(b) Missing Caps**: 0 projected calls despite 24 timers means event-driven triggers completely bypass static analysis. Centralized, system-wide token-bucket limits are missing.

**(c) Cost Blow-up (2x)**: If event rates double, Gemini safely hits its hardcoded 100-call cap. But if Anthropic’s `/tmp` cap fails, a 2x timer trigger yields 48 Opus calls/minute (~$138k/day blow-up).

**(d) Unnoticed Burn**: Anthropic. Operators will trust the $0.00 projection while missing lockfiles silently drain funds.

**Forecast if wrong**: If `/tmp` is rigorously persistent, Gemini becomes the
ai_consensus.anthropic:
**Audit verdict: "LOW" is misleading — projected=0 because no sources were discovered, not because nothing fires.**

(a) **Runaway loop:** 24 timers discovered, 0 mapped to projections. Any timer that calls Anthropic outside the `/tmp/audit_check_ai_last_run.txt` path bypasses the lockfile — `/tmp` also wipes on reboot, re-arming Opus ($2/fire) repeatedly on crash-loops.

(b) **Missing caps:** Anthropic cap=1/day relies on one lockfile, no token/-spend ceiling, no Haiku cap. Gemini's 100/day lives only in `gemma4_helper.py`; any new caller importing the SDK directly skips it.

(c) **2× rate:** Opus → $4/day trivially; Gemini → 200 calls silently (still "headroom").

(d) **Most likely silent burn:** Gemini via `audit_fire_debate` — debate loops multiply calls per fire and stay under the 100
ai_consensus.grok:
**Blow-up scenarios:**

(a) **Runaway loops**: 24 discovered timers + recursive `audit_fire_debate` could loop Anthropic calls if lockfile race or `/tmp` cleanup occurs.

(b) **Missing caps**: Gemini cap only guards two functions; any new caller bypasses the 100/day limit entirely.

(c) **2x blow-up**: Anthropic doubling to 2 calls/day (lockfile fail) = $4/day ($1,460/yr). Gemini 200 calls remains cheap.

(d) **Stealth burn**: Anthropic is most dangerous — one silent Opus call every few hours via broken lockfile stays invisible until the bill hits.

Lockfile is brittle single point of failure.
